speaker: MATT
raw_text: -- from the sides of the tree. Everyone roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead and roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have no respect for anyone who says we're not so different.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And shitting black diarrhea out of the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the arrow hits his form as he's being pulled up-- (gasps)-- you hear the air escape like he had the wind knocked out of him. His glowing yellow eyes blink, blink, and his jaw kind of hangs open. There in the low-lit darkness. And you watch as more of that black, thick tar just begins to pour from his mouth onto the ground, splattering and seeping into the floor. His jaw slowly closes. "Then I am to be alone." He reaches his hand up, and you watch--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- as these vines reveal and give to him this large curved bow that he grasps in his hand. The other vines curl around his body, and you can see his form begin to build, like his actual body begins to grow as the vines become part of him. His face of flesh disappears behind an exterior of dark and gnarled wood. The voice grows deep as he all of a sudden withdraws into the side of the tree itself, and then bursts forth, landing on the ground. Not an elf-like entity, but some sort of gnarled root-like tree creature, holding Fenthras in one hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was that bad? Should I have not attacked him?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, no, he was being terrible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Become another memory here and keep me company in oblivion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The two dead trees quake and begin to lift up--
